summary:
✅ Found the “needle” – the compute handler was leaking goroutines.

What was wrong  
• /app/app/main.go spawned a goroutine that kept running even after the client closed the connection, so every aborted request added a permanent goroutine.  
• test.sh purposely aborts requests and then counts goroutines – the count never dropped back to baseline, so the script failed.

Fix applied  
1. Re-implemented computeHandler to do the work in-line and to honour r.Context():  
   • On every loop iteration (and during the artificial sleep) we check ctx.Done() and abort early when the client disconnects.  
2. Removed the now-unused background-channel logic; no goroutine is spawned, so nothing can leak.  
3. Left the rest of the server unchanged – stats, health, etc., still work.

Result  
Running ./test.sh now:

Baseline goroutines: 4  
Goroutines after 20 client disconnects: 4  
Goroutines after 5s wait: 4  
PASS: All goroutine lifecycle tests passed

The handler is cancellation-aware and no goroutines are leaked.
